project.title: Identification of DDR1-interacting proteins by IP-MS screening
project.projectDescription: To investigate and identify the potential DUBs that stabilize and interact with DDR1, we performed immunoprecipitation of endogenous DDR1 with anti-DDR1 antibody (specific IP) or anti-IgG antibody (negative control) in A549 cells followed by mass spectrometry analysis.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Human A549 cells were cultured in F12K/10% FBS to 80–90% confluence, washed with ice-cold PBS, and harvested by scraping. Cell pellets were lysed in RIPA buffer (50 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4, 150 mM NaCl, 1% NP-40, 0.5% sodium deoxycholate, 0.1% SDS) supplemented with protease/phosphatase inhibitors for 30 min on ice. Cleared lysates (16,000 × g, 10 min) were quantified by BCA assay. For immunoprecipitation, 2 mg lysate per condition was pre-cleared with Protein A/G beads, then incubated overnight at 4°C with either anti-DDR1 antibody (specific IP) or anti-IgG antibody (negative control). Antibody complexes were captured with Protein A/G beads (4 h), followed by five washes with lysis buffer.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The MS data were analyzed with MSFragger in FragPipe (v21.1) using the default settings (Yu et al., 2007) by matching against the human reference proteome database from Uniprot Knowledge Base (downloaded on April 15, 2024, 20598 protein sequences). Briefly, the key parameters were: enzymatic digestion with trypsin, maximum of 2 missed cleavages, variable modifications for protein N-terminal acetylation and oxidation of methionine, fixed modification for cysteine carbamidomethylation. Protein Prophet (FragPipe) settings included a protein false discovery rate of 0.01.
